title: Endor Moon Hoax
content:
{{Top|leg}}
The '''Endor Moon Hoax''' was a hoax exposed by writer [[Ebenn Q3 Baobab/Legends|Ebenn Q3 Baobab]].<ref name="Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide">[[Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide (real-life book)|''Galactic Phrase Book & Travel Guide'']]</ref>

The hoax claimed that the [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Endor (planet)/Legends|Endor]] has previously been destroyed in some manner of calamity. This notion was easily refuted by its continued existence in orbit of its star, [[Ibleam]], and of the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor's]] position in orbit of the planet.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
The planet Endor was never visible in any scenes in [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] set on the forest moon's surface. [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|The novelization]] claims that Endor was destroyed some time earlier, and that the moon now orbits its star in a planetary orbit of its own.

[[Curtis Saxton]]'s [[Curtis Saxton|Star Wars Technical Commentaries]] [http://www.theforce.net/swtc/orbs.html#endor article] described the Sanctuary Moon's gas giant as absent during [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].
